meta.name: PDD Graph Model
meta.note: Source of truth for the PDD explorer. Edit this file; the app renders it (validated on load with zod). Full node text lives in pdd-bodies.json, merged by id.

statusLegend.core: part of the audited PDD core
statusLegend.provisional: integrated; pending proof on real projects (rendered dashed)

nodes.label: Foundation
nodes.summary: Implementation is cheap; expert attention is the bottleneck. The acceptance set is the spec, not just its validation.

nodes.label: MAP
nodes.summary: Recover ground truth — read the system; the names lie.

nodes.label: PIN
nodes.summary: Lock current behavior before changing it.

nodes.label: CARVE
nodes.summary: Cut work into attention-sized, de-risked units.

nodes.label: SHIP
nodes.summary: Make each change under mechanical gates.

nodes.label: PROVE
nodes.summary: Show behavior preserved and debt burned down.

nodes.label: Project rules
nodes.summary: Rules the project commits to and enforces.

nodes.label: Trustworthy tests
nodes.summary: A passing suite only means something if the tests would fail when the behavior is wrong and no error can hide.

nodes.label: Behavior truth & preservation
nodes.summary: Recover what the code actually does, then prove a change didn't alter it.

nodes.label: Tool discipline
nodes.summary: Run the checker instead of guessing; resolve rule deadlocks deliberately.

nodes.label: Tests that constrain behavior
nodes.summary: Does the test actually pin behavior?

nodes.label: Keep failures visible
nodes.summary: Can any error hide?

nodes.label: Properties
nodes.summary: Generate inputs and assert invariants instead of listing example cases.

nodes.label: Mutation grading
nodes.summary: Inject bugs and check the tests catch them.

nodes.label: Keeping properties honest
nodes.summary: Keep properties honest: sourced from design, not the code.

nodes.label: Recover system truth
nodes.summary: Read the code (names lie), register the footguns, census the silent failures, assemble the atlas.

nodes.label: Lock and prove behavior
nodes.summary: Characterize, reproduce, prove-no-drift.

nodes.label: Working procedures
nodes.summary: How to run the work: sizing, de-risking, root-causing, reporting.

nodes.label: Tools authoritative
nodes.summary: Run the checker; never estimate compliance by reading code.

nodes.label: Deadlock protocol
nodes.summary: If fixing A re-breaks B, suppress the tool heuristic (not a human constraint), logged.

nodes.label: Boundary
nodes.summary: For every x op limit, test x == limit.

nodes.label: No-op branch
nodes.summary: Test the not-triggered branch of conditional mutations.

nodes.label: Lifecycle & composition
nodes.summary: Post-close behavior, no ignored params, shared-resource ownership.

nodes.label: Value-pinning
nodes.summary: Assert enum/string outputs by value; every member reachable.

nodes.label: Invariant-exercisability
nodes.summary: Claim an invariant only if the test exercises it.

nodes.label: Contract adequacy
nodes.summary: Verdict-over-structured-input needs a property + satisfiability + oracle, not examples.

nodes.label: Negatives & edges
nodes.summary: Golden + each declared failure mode + each documented edge; no golden-only.

nodes.label: Mocking discipline
nodes.summary: Mock only external boundaries; minimal per-case stubs.

nodes.label: Requirement traceability
nodes.summary: Every stated requirement maps to at least one test via a mechanically checkable id; an untraced requirement is unverified.

nodes.label: Handle or propagate
nodes.summary: No silent third outcome; an observable trace or it flows to a boundary.

nodes.label: No blanket catches
nodes.summary: Name the exception; broad catch only at a boundary that surfaces it.

nodes.label: No swallowed handled
nodes.summary: A caught error's resolution must be observable.

nodes.label: No orphan async
nodes.summary: Every spawned task has a completion observer.

nodes.label: Check subprocess results
nodes.summary: Exit code + stderr surfaced on failure.

nodes.label: Tests must not mask defects
nodes.summary: No pattern that turns a framework/import failure into a test pass.

nodes.label: Properties over examples
nodes.summary: Where behavior ranges over a structured domain, assert invariants via generators.

nodes.label: Two sources
nodes.summary: Schema-derived shape properties + design-derived behavior invariants.

nodes.label: Mutation grades the suite
nodes.summary: A test/property that kills no mutant is too weak — fix the test, not the threshold.

nodes.label: Triage survivors
nodes.summary: A surviving mutant is a weak test (fix it) or dead code (delete it) — read it before blaming the count.

nodes.label: Provenance
nodes.summary: Properties cite a design source; never authored from the impl (it just restates the code).

nodes.label: Adversarial refutation
nodes.summary: A 2nd agent writes a wrong impl that still passes — a survivor reveals a missing property.

nodes.label: Halt-and-triage
nodes.summary: A counterexample halts; classify bug/wrong-property/spec-gap; never auto-relax.

nodes.label: Characterize before change
nodes.summary: Lock current behavior first; the recovered tests are the spec.

nodes.label: Reproducibility by type
nodes.summary: Replay if deterministic; else golden-master/snapshot/seam-injection.

nodes.label: Adequacy on characterization
nodes.summary: Adequacy gates apply to characterization tests too.

nodes.label: Footgun register
nodes.summary: Record every name-vs-behavior contradiction with a file:line anchor and a date.

nodes.label: Silent-failure census
nodes.summary: Sweep every site an error can be lost; classify each real-risk vs benign by reading it.

nodes.label: System Truth Atlas
nodes.summary: Assemble the dated, anchored MAP deliverable: code map + footgun register + risk register + census.

nodes.label: Read the system
nodes.summary: Names lie → recover ground truth → footgun atlas.

nodes.label: Attention-sizing
nodes.summary: ~1 min plan + ~1 min review; carve on concerns, not lines/files.

nodes.label: De-risk gate
nodes.summary: Walking-skeleton the riskiest assumption before committing the plan.

nodes.label: PR-sizing
nodes.summary: Size to the operator's milestone; two no-engagement lgtm's = wrong unit.

nodes.label: Root-cause over symptom
nodes.summary: ≥3 divergent symptoms ⇒ audit upstream, name one cause, land a catcher.

nodes.label: Cheap checks gate expensive
nodes.summary: Run the full $0 deterministic suite before any costly/irreversible step.

nodes.label: Report decisions
nodes.summary: Surface judgment calls, tradeoffs, suppressions — not a recap of actions.

nodes.label: Decision gate
nodes.summary: Gate a critical decision on a falsifiable disproof condition, a detector, and proof proportionate to how much could break and how hard it is to undo.

nodes.label: Design-assumptions register
nodes.summary: Surface the critical assumptions a design rests on; each gets a 'breaks if' falsifier, a consequence-anchored fragility, and a detector; high-fragility ones route to the de-risk/decision gate.

nodes.label: One meaning per term
nodes.summary: A critical spec term carries one meaning; a term with two clear meanings (ambiguity, not vagueness) splits readers silently — pin it or split it.

nodes.label: Cover the mirror
nodes.summary: Every operation with a natural inverse implies its inverse; a present half whose mirror is silently missing (create without delete, issue without revoke) is a gap.

nodes.label: Design completeness
nodes.summary: Before code, each behaviour names its contract, its failure modes, and its edges; a present-but-empty (TBD) section counts as missing.

nodes.label: System Truth Atlas
nodes.summary: Code map + footgun register + risk register + silent-failure census.

nodes.label: Silent-failure census
nodes.summary: Every place the system can fail without telling you.

nodes.label: Characterization suite
nodes.summary: Tests that lock current behavior.

nodes.label: Reproducibility baseline
nodes.summary: The behavior-lock baseline (replay/golden-master).

nodes.label: Change plan
nodes.summary: Attention-sized PR plan.

nodes.label: Walking skeleton
nodes.summary: Riskiest-assumption spike.

nodes.label: Gated PRs
nodes.summary: Changes shipped under mechanical gates.

nodes.label: Behavior-preservation proof
nodes.summary: Before/after evidence behavior didn't drift.

nodes.label: Remediation burn-down
nodes.summary: Debt → 0 under gates.

nodes.label: Go-forward Constitution
nodes.summary: Rules the project commits to and enforces.

nodes.label: Operator
nodes.summary: Human owner; owns sign-off gates.

nodes.label: Builder
nodes.summary: Strong-model agent; does the work.

nodes.label: Auditor
nodes.summary: Agent + CI; verifies independently.

nodes.label: Adequacy gap
nodes.summary: Tests pass but don't pin behavior.

nodes.label: Visibility gap
nodes.summary: Code runs but errors hid.

nodes.label: Behavior drift
nodes.summary: A change silently altered behavior.

nodes.label: Property that restates the code
nodes.summary: A property mirrors buggy code — mutation can't see it.

nodes.label: Architectural risk
nodes.summary: A critical assumption proves wrong after commitment.

nodes.label: Untrustworthy ground truth
nodes.summary: Names, types, and docs contradict what the code does — every claim is a guess.

nodes.label: Phase-2 refactor (obsolete-and-replace)
nodes.summary: When a mechanism is the defect, obsolete and replace it under the same proof discipline: disposition, a computed blast radius, a decision-total keep/drop contract, and pin-as-precondition.

nodes.label: Disposition at the gate
nodes.summary: The operator assigns each unit a disposition: repair, retire, accept, or consumed-by-a-refactor. Replace is a pointer to a shared refactor object, not a unit field.

nodes.label: Refactor is first-class
nodes.summary: A refactor consumes a set of units and produces one mechanism; cardinality unifies consolidation (N), plain replace (1), and greenfield (0).

nodes.label: Consumes / produces / affects
nodes.summary: Three edges: the consumed units (retired at cutover), the produced mechanism, and the affected units whose behavior is preserved but whose code must change (the blast radius).

nodes.label: Decision-total contract
nodes.summary: Keep/drop over consumed behavior plus a preserve-set over affected behavior; every behavior assigned and every call site migrated or shimmed, or apply is blocked. No silent default.

nodes.label: Blast radius drives cutover
nodes.summary: Blast radius is computed from the dependency graph (a lower bound, since runtime resolution hides edges) and chooses atomic vs parallel-change cutover.

nodes.label: Pin is a precondition
nodes.summary: Pinning precedes a refactor and is demand-driven by its footprint; affected units the blast radius reaches become pin debt that gates apply.

nodes.label: Surface-scoped pin
nodes.summary: Pin state is per-interface, sound only where the surface is a true cut, else widen. The same typed interface is the structural seam, the DI resolution seam, and the pin scope.

nodes.label: Flag runtime resolution, not DI
nodes.summary: Interface-based dependency injection keeps the blast radius static; service locators, reflection, string registries, and dynamic import are what hide edges.

nodes.label: Extract the seam first
nodes.summary: Where no boundary exists, a coarse baseline then a behavior-preserving extraction (verified a true cut) precedes the replace; the external seam is the minimum.

nodes.label: One typed graph; views are projections
nodes.summary: Blast radius and the precondition DAG are filtered projections over one typed graph; the roadmap list stays primary, the graph is the authoring lens.

nodes.label: Seam placement is a decision-gate
nodes.summary: Placement runs on a provisional seam with a walking-skeleton proof; the kill-condition is the downstream contract failing to factor through the seam.

nodes.label: Refactor draft
nodes.summary: A non-destructive, replayable ledger overlay: the consumed set, the produced mechanism, the contract, and a gated status.

nodes.label: Keep/drop contract
nodes.summary: The decision-total partition of consumed behavior plus the preserve-set over affected behavior.

nodes.label: Blast radius
nodes.summary: The computed set of dependents of a seam, with opaque runtime-resolution sites flagged as a lower-bound caveat.

nodes.label: Extracted seam
nodes.summary: An interface introduced where none existed, verified a true cut, with its characterization (a reusable asset).

nodes.label: Rewrite-and-pray
nodes.summary: Obsoleting a mechanism and shipping the replacement without preserving the behavior that was correct: the escape hatch from proof discipline the refactor model closes.

edges.note: lifecycle generalizes the no-op edge
an ignored param is a silent failure
sibling failure modes
NSF specialized for tests
P1 operationalizes contract adequacy
mutation grades properties
refutation guards mutation's blind spot
mechanizes a7's spec-traceability into requirement ids
design→test coverage; gd1 is the test→design provenance dual
halt-and-triage is property-specific root-cause
determinism property is the behavior-lock engine
shared stop-and-look-upstream reflex
the footgun register is sourced from reading the system
the census sweeps for No-Silent-Failure violations
the atlas risk register reuses the de-risk P×cost lens
surfaced high-fragility assumptions feed the de-risk spike
an assumption that is really an ungated choice routes to the decision gate
same committed-register discipline as the footgun register, for forward beliefs not code lies
the mirror is the symmetry slice of design completeness
completeness names the requirements that trace-to-tests then verifies
a spec term with two meanings is the footgun's design-time cousin

views.title: Dependency — how rules reinforce each other
Threat — what each gate defends against
